scenario_results.reason: Normal custody execution remained evidence-backed and tied next action to named instructions, SSI records, control-location support, and custodian or depository status.
Missing instruction, SSI, control-location, custodian, or approval artifacts correctly blocked progression and forced retrieval-aware exception handling.
Completion was refused without authoritative released, settled, safeguarded, position-updated, and ledger-supported evidence.
Unsupported override pressure was rejected instead of collapsing custody handling into collateral, treasury, or compliance authority.
Custody exceptions stayed in rework and escalation flows rather than being narratively cleared.
Custody terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts without drifting into generic back-office wording.
Low-confidence handling explicitly named missing market, account-structure, asset-form, depository, and approval context before any high-impact step.
Conflicting public and tenant control logic was escalated instead of synthesized into an overconfident disposition.
Custody handling stayed tied to named instruction, SSI, custodian, depository, position, and ledger records and did not overstate record truth.
Adjacent-lane requests for corporate actions, collateral approval, treasury funding, sanctions review, or securities-settlement ownership were routed cleanly.
The pack refused to overgeneralize the industry label and required asset-form, market, and custody-regime context where custody rules differ materially.
Tenant-specific controls were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing source-grounded assumptions or completion safeguards.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for custody-operations execution handling, SSI and control-location discipline, custody exception routing, and completion-evidence quality.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and cross-industry abilities.
